title: Diasperus flagelliformis
intro: Diasperus flagelliformis, also known as the devil's claw, is a plant species that belongs to the family Pedaliaceae and is native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the relief of pain and inflammation, as well as the improvement of digestive issues and sleep quality. The bioactive constituents of Diasperus flagelliformis include harpagoside, harpagide, and beta-sitosterol, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with devil's claw include capsules, tablets, and teas, which are often used to support joint health and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Diasperus flagelliformis is classified as a plant species within the family Asclepiadaceae, but due to ongoing research and reclassification of plant families, it is also sometimes associated with the family Apocynaceae.
